audit_items.proof_trace: The `ber_strdup(cvalue)` calls at lines 167/184/204/224/244 can only return NULL on OOM (cvalue is already verified non-NULL before each call), and no dereference of the result occurs within `handle_private_option()` itself. In `main()`, `vc_sasl_mech` is guarded by `if (vc_sasl_mech)` at lines 317 and 355 before any SASL path executes; `lutil_sasl_defaults()` (sasl.c:80-97) accepts NULL for all parameters via ternary + `ldap_get_option()` fallback, so NULL realm/authcid are handled gracefully. OOM is not attacker-controllable through command-line arguments of this client tool.
